subject: Mathematics
title: Abundant and Deficient Numbers
description: A 60-second lesson on Abundant and Deficient Numbers.

hook.text: Most numbers are either generous or stingy when you add up their divisors.

buildup.text: 12 is abundant: its proper divisors 1+2+3+4+6 sum to 16, more than 12 itself.

discovery.text: 8 is deficient: its proper divisors 1+2+4 sum to only 7, less than 8.

twist.text: About 75% of all integers are deficient. Abundant numbers are surprisingly rare.

climax.text: Perfect numbers sit at the exact boundary: neither abundant nor deficient.

punchline.text: Every number has a personality: generous, stingy, or perfect.

quiz.question: What makes a number abundant?
options: Its proper divisors sum to more than itself
It has more than five factors
